creator: sbz@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Fri, Mar 15, 2013 at 01:35:10PM +0100, Christoph Mallon wrote:
> This has nothing to do with git.
> send-pr inserts these lines to mark the begin and end of attached files (no matter whether they are diffs or not).
> I have to check, whether the web interface to submit PRs does something different, which would be very inconsistent.
>

Correct, i just look in send-pr, line below in gnu/usr.bin/send-pr/send-pr.sh

--- $PRETTY_NAME begins here ---
`cat \"$2\"`
--- $PRETTY_NAME ends here ---
"

> My change does not care for the patch format or any other aspect of the attached file.
> It simply uses the markers inserted by send-pr to identify files.
> The GNATS web interface fails to identify these lines properly.
> It skips every other file.
> An alternative solution would be to fix the web interface to correctly identify these lines in all cases.

The web interface code is in htdocs/cgi/dosendpr.cgi, the submission program is
not send-pr, it's sendmail, so these lines aren't append.

Turns out, the patch break the tool after application. As an example:

% ./getpatch ports/176257
[+] Fetching patch for pr ports/176257
[-] No patch found

% ./getpatch.orig ports/176257
[+] Fetching patch for pr ports/176257
[+] knock.diff created

As modify the web interface is not the solution, do you think you can parse it
without 'begins' and 'ends' indicators ?

Regards

--
Sofian Brabez
